topic.what: Firsthand accounts from scientists who observed gastric spiral bacteria from 1892-1982, documenting the many 'near-misses' before Marshall-Warren.
topic.how: Compiled first-person narratives from researchers including Bizzozero, Freedberg, and others who observed but did not pursue gastric bacteria.
topic.why_matters: Documents how paradigmatic assumptions (sterile stomach, acid-centric disease model) suppressed empirical findings for decades — essential for understanding scientific resistance to new evidence.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of documented pre-Marshall observations of gastric bacteria

good_for: Understanding scientific resistance to paradigm change

novelty: Unique compilation of firsthand near-miss accounts
Documents the sociology of scientific resistance in gastroenterology
open_problems: Retrospective accounts may be colored by hindsight
Some contributors' roles may be overstated
